---
title: "PySDM Seeding Lab"
lang: en
page-layout: full
title-block-style: none
listing:
  contents: posts
  sort: "date desc"
  type: default
  page-size: 6
  categories: true
  feed:
    title: "PySDM Seeding Lab"
    description: "Notes on learning PySDM, building a reproducible lab, and cloud-seeding experiments"
---

::: {.home-intro}
<div class="home-kicker">PySDM · Cloud microphysics · Open notebook</div>
<h1>Computing clouds,<br><em>documenting experiments.</em></h1>
<p class="home-deck">I learn PySDM and build reproducible experimental environments. Cloud-seeding hypotheses are tested with code and figures, while the questions, failures, and results are documented along the way.</p>
<div class="home-actions">
  <a href="learn-pysdm.html">Read the first note <span aria-hidden="true">↗</span></a>
  <a href="projects.html">About the project <span aria-hidden="true">→</span></a>
</div>
:::

## Three research tracks {.section-heading}

:::: {.track-list}
::: {.track-item}
<div class="track-index">01</div>
### Learn PySDM

Build a foundation step by step, from the Super-Droplet Method to PySDM objects and official examples.

[Browse learning notes](learn-pysdm.qmd)
:::

::: {.track-item}
<div class="track-index">02</div>
### Build the Lab

Document environments, data, configurations, and validation rules so anyone can reproduce the same calculations.

[Browse build notes](build-the-lab.qmd)
:::

::: {.track-item}
<div class="track-index">03</div>
### Experiments

Use sensitivity studies and comparative figures to explore when and why seeding hypotheses lead to different outcomes.

[Browse experiments](experiments.qmd)
:::
::::

## Recent notes {.section-heading}
